title: Señoritis

jokes.text: And if you do my Spanish, I'll teach you how to French.
jokes.setup: Maeby offering to reward Perfecto for doing homework
jokes.punchline: The triple entendre mixing language learning with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever triple wordplay with 'Spanish' (homework), 'French' (language/kissing), delivered with character-appropriate boldness

jokes.text: Does that include the crew?
jokes.setup: Teacher telling students to close their eyes if they passed
jokes.punchline: Student asking about the documentary crew filming them
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta joke acknowledging the show's documentary format within the narrative

jokes.text: Well, if it's dumb to stay behind with you, call me a dummy.
jokes.setup: Perfecto failed the test and has to stay for tutoring
jokes.punchline: His cheesy romantic line using 'dummy' to flirt
jokes.explanation: Standard cheesy pickup line but fits Perfecto's earnest character

jokes.text: We don't say 'dummy,' and unfortunately you two alge-tards are going to need some special tutoring.
jokes.setup: Teacher being politically correct about 'dummy'
jokes.punchline: Immediately using the offensive portmanteau 'alge-tards'
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony and creative wordplay - teacher's hypocrisy is perfectly executed

jokes.text: What kind of loser spends his free time tutoring high school kids? - This kind.
jokes.setup: Maeby dismissively asking what kind of loser tutors high schoolers
jokes.punchline: Cut to George Michael, the exact kind of loser she described
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and structure with immediate visual punchline

jokes.text: George Michael? Huh, what is this? Maeby, what are you doing here?
jokes.setup: George Michael expecting to tutor a high school student
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's his cousin who should have graduated years ago
jokes.explanation: Builds on established family dysfunction and creates awkward tension

jokes.text: But, wait, why are you in high school? Didn't we graduate together five years ago?
jokes.setup: George Michael confused about timeline
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's been 5 years since they graduated
jokes.explanation: Establishes the absurd timeline of Maeby still being in high school

jokes.text: It had been seven minutes.
jokes.setup: Maeby claiming she's been lying there for an hour and a half
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dry correction that it was actually seven minutes
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator undermining character delusions with precise timing

jokes.text: ♪ No, it's just a phallus... eee! ♪
jokes.setup: Parents supposedly too busy to notice Maeby
jokes.punchline: They're actually singing about male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Absurd and unexpected - perfectly captures the parents' obliviousness

jokes.text: George Michael wasn't a very good kisser. It seems he both used his tongue to fend off the intruding tongue, but also laterally moved his lower jaw while constricting his lower lip.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Maeby didn't want to get more serious
jokes.punchline: Overly clinical description of terrible kissing technique
jokes.explanation: Hilariously over-detailed technical analysis makes awkward situation even more uncomfortable

jokes.text: And that doesn't make me hotter to you?
jokes.setup: George Michael saying his dad doesn't want them together
jokes.punchline: Maeby's expectation that forbidden fruit should be more appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Maeby's manipulative nature and George Michael's immunity

jokes.text: We're a couple of, uh, forces of nature, but... - What? - Forces of nature.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to sound confident about their sexuality
jokes.punchline: Having to repeat the awkward phrase when Maeby doesn't hear it
jokes.explanation: George Michael's awkwardness compounded by having to repeat his failed cool line

jokes.text: I've got two whole months to flunk. That's got to disappoint them.
jokes.setup: Maeby deciding to get her parents' attention by graduating
jokes.punchline: Her twisted logic that flunking will disappoint them more
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Maeby's warped relationship with her parents

jokes.text: It's Maeby's 'Arrested Development.'
jokes.setup: Standard AD opening about family whose future was cancelled
jokes.punchline: Title drop that works both literally (Maeby's delayed development) and meta-textually
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke that recontextualizes the entire show premise for this character-focused episode

jokes.text: Right. Okay, do we have to pay them in money or in rice? If they're in the union, you have to pay with rice.
jokes.setup: Discussing payment for extras in India
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic absurdity of union rules requiring rice payment
jokes.explanation: Specific absurdist humor about film industry bureaucracy abroad

jokes.text: You'd think someone would tell her to shave her arms, the poor thing.
jokes.setup: Maeby commenting on someone off-screen
jokes.punchline: Casual cruelty delivered as if it's helpful advice
jokes.explanation: Shows Maeby's Hollywood callousness disguised as concern

jokes.text: Gangie on the Ganges? That doesn't even make sense.
jokes.setup: The movie title 'Gangie on the Ganges'
jokes.punchline: Maeby's awareness that the pun doesn't work geographically
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about forced wordplay in movie titles

jokes.text: Pretty makeup or scary makeup? Scary. Well, I think we have the crew that did The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills. Perfect.
jokes.setup: Needing scary makeup for the horror movie
jokes.punchline: The Real Housewives crew being perfect for making people look scary
jokes.explanation: Sharp dig at reality TV and the entertainment industry's beauty standards

jokes.text: I'm so full of passion. You are so full of (bleep). Yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Lindsay seeking spiritual advice from a shaman
jokes.punchline: The shaman's blunt assessment being mistaken for spiritual wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - what sounds like wisdom is actually an insult

jokes.text: She's away at boarding school in England. Is that where we left it?
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming Maeby is away at boarding school
jokes.punchline: Narrator questioning if that's the story they're going with
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about narrative consistency and parental neglect

jokes.text: Please don't squeeze the shaman.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to hug the spiritual advisor
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Don't squeeze the Charmin' toilet paper ads
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference that works but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Thank you, Maeby, that's actually a compliment in India. My own fault.
jokes.setup: Maeby trying to insult her mother by calling her full of shit
jokes.punchline: The insult backfiring due to cultural differences
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of Maeby's attempt at cruelty through cultural context

jokes.text: Senior year! No? No one cares?
jokes.setup: Maeby announcing she's going back for senior year
jokes.punchline: Her desperate plea for attention falling flat
jokes.explanation: Painful moment that perfectly captures Maeby's desperate need for parental attention

jokes.text: Why would they preempt 'Babies Having Babies' for a turkey cartoon?
jokes.setup: Family watching TV on Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Tobias dismissing Thanksgiving specials as inferior to teen pregnancy reality TV
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities that highlight the family's dysfunction

jokes.text: having celebrated it one week earlier when they found that duck in the cupboard, tried to cook it alive, got scared, and then ended up eating it at a Chinese restaurant
jokes.setup: Explaining their unconventional Thanksgiving celebration
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous chain of events with the duck
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating absurdity that captures the family's incompetence

jokes.text: Maeby, your daddy's not going to be around anymore. Excuse me, I swallowed a bug there. Uh, your daddy's not going to be around anymore.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to deliver serious news about leaving
jokes.punchline: His dramatic moment being ruined by swallowing a bug
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy undermining Tobias's attempt at gravitas

jokes.text: Also, Tobias, you got a callback for something called 'The Big Bang Theory.'
jokes.setup: Delivering bad news about foreclosure
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Tobias's callback for the hit sitcom
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about competing shows and Tobias's perpetual near-misses

jokes.text: Yeah. 'Cause I feel like we'll be here forever, and I have a meeting. Um, you're fired.
jokes.setup: Executive about to deliver important news
jokes.punchline: Casual firing delivered with complete indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly callous Hollywood executive behavior delivered with comic timing

jokes.text: Actually, I was thinking I wasn't even gonna have to fire you 'cause it's been on Deadline all day.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining the firing
jokes.punchline: Revealing he expected her to find out from trade publications
jokes.explanation: Escalates the casual cruelty with industry-specific humor

jokes.text: Yeah, it does. I told them.
jokes.setup: Maeby asking how they found out she lacks a diploma
jokes.punchline: Ron casually admitting he's the one who exposed her
jokes.explanation: Unexpected betrayal delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: which is exactly what she decided to do... / What? / ...at her grandparents' penthouse.
jokes.setup: Narrator saying Maeby had squat and decided to do that
jokes.punchline: Visual of her literally squatting/occupying the penthouse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun execution typical of AD's wordplay sophistication

jokes.text: Pieces of (bleep).
jokes.setup: Maeby trying to leave the model home
jokes.punchline: Discovering the infamous Bluth construction quality
jokes.explanation: Pays off long-running gag about Bluth homes being impossible to escape

jokes.text: That is it-- this is like living in 1999.
jokes.setup: Maeby frustrated with lack of internet
jokes.punchline: Treating 1999 like ancient history
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing generational perspective on technology

jokes.text: And we're not moving in; we're occupying a space with central air.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining their living situation
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of squatting focused on amenities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay prioritizing comfort over truth in euphemistic language

jokes.text: Oh, that's good-- I'm gonna try and work that in there. Yeah-- no, I think it's in there.
jokes.setup: George Michael pointing out that her plan is itself immature
jokes.punchline: Maeby treating it like dialogue she can incorporate
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where character acknowledges the performance aspect of her rebellion

jokes.text: P-Hound. I thought it was the other one. No, it-it was P-Hound, yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael hearing about their roommate
jokes.punchline: The ongoing confusion about which roommate is which
jokes.explanation: Continues running gag about interchangeable roommates

jokes.text: Well, it's just a Boolean-driven aggregation, really, of what programmers call 'hacker-traps...'
jokes.setup: Maeby asking about his software
jokes.punchline: George Michael's incomprehensible technical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's technical knowledge and social awkwardness

jokes.text: You should see his Facebook page. Doesn't have a friend on it.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to showcase George Michael's privacy software
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently revealing his son's social isolation
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor - Michael's pride inadvertently highlighting George Michael's loneliness

jokes.text: Of course, that last part had nothing to do with Fakeblock...
jokes.setup: Michael's claim about George Michael's empty Facebook page
jokes.punchline: Narrator clarifying it's just because he has no friends
jokes.explanation: Narrator's dry clarification makes the sad truth even funnier

jokes.text: Can you imagine what kind of loser pretends to be in high school when they're in their 20s? Well, it's pretty gutsy, right?
jokes.setup: Students talking about undercover cops at school
jokes.punchline: Maeby trying to defend herself while being the exact person they're describing
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Maeby defending herself while being caught in the exact behavior being mocked

jokes.text: Actually, can we make it-- uh, marry me-- a Wild Turkey? I still need to see some I.D.
jokes.setup: Maeby trying to order an adult drink
jokes.punchline: Her Freudian slip revealing her desperation, followed by still needing ID
jokes.explanation: Slip reveals subconscious desires while practical problem persists

jokes.text: I'm supposed to believe you're 17? Oh, no, sorry. That's my fake for sneaking back into high school.
jokes.setup: Bartender questioning her age on the ID
jokes.punchline: Revealing she has opposite fake IDs for opposite purposes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - most people fake being older, she fakes being younger, perfectly encapsulating her unique situation

jokes.text: These guys are making a fortune off that 'To Entrap a Local Predator' show they're on. Were. Won't let us shoot in Laguna.
jokes.setup: Commenting on successful TV show
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that they've been banned from filming locations
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about reality TV exploitation and its consequences

jokes.text: After 21, it is illegal for you to enroll in high school in the state of California. As a matter of fact, you cannot even lurk.
jokes.setup: Explaining age restrictions for high school
jokes.punchline: Uses the specific legal term 'lurk'
jokes.explanation: Great legal term usage that sounds appropriately creepy

jokes.text: I could ask you the same thing. I go to high school here. I could answer the same thing.
jokes.setup: Barry asking what Maeby's doing at high school
jokes.punchline: Both revealing they're inappropriately at high school
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing both characters are where they shouldn't be

jokes.text: What, do they got cameras in the bushes? Why would they do that? You can't even see into the locker rooms from there.
jokes.setup: Discussion about lurking laws around schools
jokes.punchline: Barry's creepy knowledge of school surveillance blind spots
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Barry's inappropriate knowledge while staying in character

jokes.text: And she wouldn't even let me get a cat.
jokes.setup: Maeby dealing with an ostrich in the penthouse
jokes.punchline: Her complaint about not being allowed a normal pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition between exotic dangerous bird and simple house cat

jokes.text: And she wouldn't even let me get a cat.
jokes.setup: After encountering an ostrich in the penthouse
jokes.punchline: Complains parents wouldn't let her have a normal pet
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between exotic birds and simple pets

jokes.text: Oh, my God! / Here, that way, that way. / And she wouldn't even let me get a cat.
jokes.setup: Maeby encountering an ostrich at the penthouse
jokes.punchline: Complaint about not being allowed a cat while ostrich runs wild
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist juxtaposition - complaining about cat restrictions while ostrich is present

jokes.text: I'm getting an Opie? The only bigger honor would be having an award like that named after you, I guess.
jokes.setup: Maeby excited about getting an award
jokes.punchline: Her comment about the ultimate honor, not knowing what's coming
jokes.explanation: Foreshadows the lifetime achievement award twist while showing Maeby's naivety

jokes.text: You know what? I think I will do better once I get a little Mexican in me.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking what it will take for Maeby to focus
jokes.punchline: Her response with obvious double meaning about Perfecto
jokes.explanation: Obvious double entendre but fits Maeby's bold character

jokes.text: No, they only give that award away to somebody after they die or, even worse, after your career is dead.
jokes.setup: Maeby excited about her lifetime achievement award
jokes.punchline: Mort explaining the award means her career is over
jokes.explanation: Dark truth about industry awards delivered with perfect escalation

jokes.text: I think I just remember it that way 'cause it got dicey when I was sneaking back in to take a (bleep) on Sid's desk.
jokes.setup: Mort claiming his exit was graceful
jokes.punchline: Revealing the ungraceful revenge he attempted
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation from dignified exit to literal shit-talking

jokes.text: Take a hat; we got 18,000 of them.
jokes.setup: Mort promoting his new tech company Schnoodle
jokes.punchline: The massive overproduction of promotional materials for failed company
jokes.explanation: Visual representation of startup failure and misplaced optimism

jokes.text: Well, if you need a name, I'm the guy who came up with Schnoodle. How do we know each other? We don't. That's what it is.
jokes.setup: Mort offering naming advice to strangers
jokes.punchline: The awkward realization they don't actually know each other
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hollywood networking desperation

jokes.text: What, you think they invite the animals and ask them to wait while they build the zoo? No, that would be a bloodbath. Yeah, a total (bleep) bloodbath.
jokes.setup: Maeby arguing they need to promote Fakeblock before it's ready
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly violent zoo metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor that escalates perfectly and becomes surprisingly violent

jokes.text: Come on, this could be my only chance to be a Harris. A Harris? Oh, heiress. You meant... You don't pronounce the 'H.'
jokes.setup: Maeby talking about being a 'Harris'
jokes.punchline: The pronunciation confusion revealing her true meaning
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay revealing Maeby's desire for wealth and status

jokes.text: I tried to deactivate my Facebook profile. It's easier to get out of Scientology.
jokes.setup: Rebel complaining about social media
jokes.punchline: Comparing Facebook to notoriously difficult-to-leave religion
jokes.explanation: Sharp contemporary observation with effective exaggeration

jokes.text: That's my undercover cop boyfriend. I'm trying to get him in bed.
jokes.setup: Introducing Perfecto to others
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to seducing someone she thinks is a cop
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Maeby's scheme delivered with characteristic boldness

jokes.text: It's the kids from 'Modern Family.' Sorry. Rico!
jokes.setup: Perfecto trying to maintain his cover story
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by celebrity sighting mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about competing sitcom while showing character priorities

jokes.text: To be continued. You better believe it.
jokes.setup: Episode ending with 'To be continued'
jokes.punchline: Maeby responds as if she heard the narrator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment breaking fourth wall

jokes.text: Double (bleep) newsflash. I'm starting Fakeblock with George Maharis, the world's first antisocial network.
jokes.setup: Maeby's acceptance speech getting increasingly hostile
jokes.punchline: The oxymoronic 'antisocial network' concept
jokes.explanation: Clever oxymoron that perfectly captures the product's contradictory nature

jokes.text: So you can take this Opie and shove it up your (bleep), all right? Because I will (bleep) punt the next god(bleep) (bleep) who tells me I'm finished, you (bleep) (bleep)hats.
jokes.setup: Maeby's increasingly profane acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: The complete meltdown with creative profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character meltdown with escalating profanity and specific insults

jokes.text: Are you really gonna whore yourself out like that? I am not a whore. Yeah, you're a whore.
jokes.setup: Maeby confronting her mother about compromising principles
jokes.punchline: The blunt back-and-forth about prostitution
jokes.explanation: Direct confrontation with ironic undertones given what Maeby is planning

jokes.text: and became her mother's pimp.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining how Maeby got seed money
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact statement about becoming her mother's pimp
jokes.explanation: Shocking, matter-of-fact summary of deeply dysfunctional family relationship

jokes.text: And just to be clear, I want to hire her for prostitution. Yes, no, definitely. That was clear. I'm gonna charge you for that.
jokes.setup: Campaign manager making his intentions clear
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's casual acceptance and billing mindset
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prostitution delivered with business-like professionalism

jokes.text: For all I know, it's not even real! But I think this might be going through the roof! I'm calling it my first 'Hypothetical Buy.'
jokes.setup: Jim Cramer promoting Fakeblock on his show
jokes.punchline: Admitting it might not exist while still recommending it
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of financial TV and speculative investing

jokes.text: That's funny, I actually used to date a guy named Steve Holt. Ha! Steve Holt! Yeah, that's the guy.
jokes.setup: Exterminator mentioning he used to date someone named Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: The signature Steve Holt exclamation and recognition
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works both as character reference and reveals exterminator's sexuality

jokes.text: Why did we stop? / We hit a bug. / I know, I know, I've got these things very, you know, meticulously maintained. / (bleep) / There are some vultures. They might still smell Pete.
jokes.setup: Car trouble during family visit
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that vultures smell Pete, implying death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about death delivered as mundane car maintenance issue

jokes.text: Maeby, you're fired! I got her coffee.
jokes.setup: George Michael finally standing up to Maeby
jokes.punchline: Mort's oblivious interjection about mundane task
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - dramatic moment undercut by mundane interruption

jokes.text: You're never too old to stand up to a bully.
jokes.setup: Revealing what Perfecto's 'police badge' actually was
jokes.punchline: The earnest anti-bullying message in this context
jokes.explanation: Ironic given the context and reveals Perfecto's actual innocent nature

jokes.text: What kind of cop doesn't have a gun? / Okay, I'm not a cop. / But I saw your badge at the bar. / In fact, it was an anti-bullying badge.
jokes.setup: Maeby questioning Perfecto about his cop equipment
jokes.punchline: Revelation that his badge was from anti-bullying program, not police
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal deflating the cop mystique with innocent school program

jokes.text: You're never too old to stand up to a bully.
jokes.setup: Perfecto's badge that Maeby thought was police
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's just an anti-bullying program badge
jokes.explanation: Good reveal subverting expectations about the badge

jokes.text: A señor? I'm 17. Ha! You're 17? Yeah. I'm 23. Oh, that's a relief.
jokes.setup: The age confusion between Maeby and Perfecto
jokes.punchline: Both being relieved for the wrong reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - both characters think they're in the clear when they're actually in more trouble

jokes.text: Congratulations. You got your first A. Oh, thank God. This A is finally gonna get me out of high school. No, sex offender, your A is going to jail.
jokes.setup: Maeby thinking she finally got an A grade
jokes.punchline: The revelation that A stands for her statutory rape classification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay - the A she's always wanted becomes her criminal classification. Perfect culmination of the episode's themes.

jokes.text: Oh, God. I'm gonna have to live in Sudden Valley.
jokes.setup: Realizing she's in serious legal trouble
jokes.punchline: References the terrible housing development from earlier seasons
jokes.explanation: Good callback to running gag about terrible housing for sex offenders

tmdb_overview: Maeby moves out on her own and reconnects with her cousin George-Michael.
insight: Wordplay-driven episode sustains 83.5 score across 76 jokes with minimal standout moments.
